markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and parent company Take-Two Interactive have locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 release on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S, following a delay from the prior May target to allow extra polish. With roughly five months remaining until launch, trader sentiment in the “What will happen before GTA VI?” market centers on the compressed window for any listed events, from new trailers and GTA Online seasonal updates to broader entertainment milestones. Recent earnings calls reaffirmed the date without signaling further shifts, while ongoing promotional cycles and industry-wide scheduling impacts from the anticipated title continue to shape expectations. Upcoming catalysts include potential summer 2026 content drops and chart or streaming benchmarks that could resolve specific outcomes ahead of the fixed launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The string of mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, most recently on May 15, 2026, has anchored trader sentiment at 80% implied probability for no additional prison time. Repeated deadlocked juries on the core rape charge involving Jessica Mann have underscored the challenges of securing convictions years after the original 2020 verdict was overturned, while his separate California conviction faces active appeals. At 74 and in declining health, Weinstein’s legal team continues to highlight these factors alongside his existing time served since 2020. A June 24 hearing will determine whether prosecutors pursue a fourth Manhattan trial, but current momentum favors limited further exposure.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights shapes current trader consensus, with the closely contested series tied 2-2 after high-scoring affairs featuring rapid comebacks and record-setting offensive bursts. Carolina's stronger regular-season positioning, deeper playoff run with minimal losses entering the final, and home-ice advantage in remaining games support their slight edge in implied probability. Vegas counters with proven postseason resilience, including a sweep of the Avalanche and contributions from key forwards like Mitch Marner amid defensive adjustments. Recent momentum swings—driven by Jordan Staal's scoring streak for Carolina and road results—reflect the even matchup, where small edges in goaltending, special teams, or rest could shift outcomes before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks hold an 81% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Championship market due to their commanding 3-1 lead in the NBA Finals against the San Antonio Spurs. New York posted a 53-29 regular-season record, ranking third in the East with elite offensive efficiency. The Spurs, despite a 62-20 mark and Victor Wembanyama's two-way dominance, saw their title odds shorten after the Game 3 win but remain underdogs facing elimination. The wisdom of crowds in the market reflects the Knicks' strong home form and series control heading into Game 5.

The wisdom of crowds in the market reflects the Knicks' strong home form and series control heading into Game 5.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain, France, and England sit atop the World Cup winner market with closely bunched implied probabilities reflecting their status as the clearest title contenders. markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s entrenched dominance within the Chinese Communist Party, state apparatus, and Central Military Commission underpins the strong trader consensus against his removal before 2027. He has continued to direct policy, including the launch of the 15th Five-Year Plan for 2026–2030 and economic stimulus measures, while conducting further military leadership purges that analysts view as reinforcing rather than eroding his authority. Speculation about health issues or internal challenges in 2025 proved unfounded, consistent with prior dismissed rumors, and no successor has been designated ahead of the 21st Party Congress. This institutional consolidation and absence of credible succession mechanisms support the elevated implied probability that Xi will retain power through at least the end of 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the lead in trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 23.5 percent, driven by his record as California governor, national visibility from state-federal clashes, and established donor networks amid a fragmented field with no declared candidates as of June 2026. Early positioning by figures such as Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Jon Ossoff, Kamala Harris, and Josh Shapiro occurs through media appearances, donor outreach, and polling tests, highlighting differentiators like governors' proven statewide wins versus congressional or national profiles and appeals ranging from progressive to moderate. Support may consolidate after the November 2026 midterms via fundraising totals, battleground polling trends, and endorsements, though the process extends to the 2028 convention.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the lead in trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 23.5 percent, driven by his record as California governor, national visibility from state-federal clashes, and established donor networks amid a fragmented field with no declared candidates as of June 2026. Early positioning by figures such as Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Jon Ossoff, Kamala Harris, and Josh Shapiro occurs through media appearances, donor outreach, and polling tests, highlighting differentiators like governors' proven statewide wins versus congressional or national profiles and appeals ranging from progressive to moderate. Support may consolidate after the November 2026 midterms via fundraising totals, battleground polling trends, and endorsements, though the process extends to the 2028 convention.
